prerequisites: A Timely account with a workspace, since every resource belongs to a workspace identified by account_id.
Your Timely account_id (workspace ID). Retrieve account IDs by calling GET /1.1/accounts after authenticating.
A bearer token for the API. Tokens are obtained via the OAuth 2.0 flow or from the Timely docs login.
Admin access to the workspace if you need to create an OAuth application at https://app.timelyapp.com/{account_id}/oauth_applications.
A start_date to define the point from which the connector begins replicating data.

authMethods.name: Bearer token
authMethods.summary: The Timely API uses HTTP bearer authentication. Provide the token in the connector's bearer_token field; it is sent as the Authorization: Bearer {access_token} header. Tokens are obtained through Timely's OAuth 2.0 flow or from the docs login.

setupSteps.title: Obtain a bearer token
setupSteps.body: Acquire a Timely access token. Timely uses OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code flow): create an OAuth application at https://app.timelyapp.com/{account_id}/oauth_applications (admin access required), redirect users to /1.1/oauth/authorize with your client_id and redirect_uri, then POST to /1.1/oauth/token with the authorization code to receive an access token. A token can also be obtained from the Timely docs login.

setupSteps.title: Find your account_id
setupSteps.body: Every resource in Timely belongs to a workspace identified by account_id, and most endpoints require this ID in the path. After authenticating, call GET /1.1/accounts to list your account IDs and choose the workspace you want to extract from.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the account_id of the workspace to sync, paste your token into the bearer_token field, and set start_date to the date from which the connector should begin replicating records.

sources.title: Timely API Reference (Authentication and Core Concepts)
Timely API documentation (dev.timelyapp.com, redirects to developer.timely.com)
